project.title: urinary glycopeptide profile differentiates early cardiorenal risk in subjects not meeting criteria for chronic kidney disease
project.projectDescription: Early diagnosis and treatment of chronic kidney disease (CKD) is a worldwide challenge. Albuminuria is defined as urinary albumin:creatinine ratio (ACR) ≥30 mg/g. Subjects with ACR values below this cut-off and preserved renal function are considered at no cardiorenal risk, but increased risk is established even at the 10-30 mg/g ACR range, supporting the need to identify other molecular indicators for early assessment of normoalbuminuric patients at higher risk. Following our previous studies, here we aimed to stratify the normoalbuminuria range according to cardiorenal risk and identify glycoproteins and N-glycosylation sites associated to kidney damage in subclinical CKD.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Urine samples were collected in sterile recipients, centrifuged at 3000 g for 10 min and stored at -80ºC until analysis. Proteins were digested with modified trypsin (Promega) using a protein:trypsin ratio 30:1 (w/w) and using the FASP (Filter Aided Sample Prep, Pall) protocol. To quantify the glycoproteins, tryptic N-glycopeptides were isolated by a FASP-Based N-linked Glycopeptide Capture Method (N-Glyco-FASP).
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The resulting spectra were analyzed with Proteome Discoverer (2.1.0.81 version, Thermo Fisher Scientific) and SEQUEST-HT (Thermo Fisher Scientific). The Uniprot Database was used for protein identification, containing all sequences from humans and contaminants (May 14ᵗʰ, 2016, 70611 entries). Carbamidomethylcysteine and iTRAQ modifications at the N-terminal and Lys’s residues were defined as fixed modifications, and methionine oxidation and Asn deamidation were selected as dynamic modifications. Peptide identification was carried out using the probability ratio method21, and the false discovery rate (FDR) was calculated using inverted databases and the refined method22, with an additional filtering for precursor mass tolerance of 15 ppm23. Identified peptides had an FDR ≤1%. Differential quantitation between clinical groups Differential quantitation was performed at two levels: glycoproteins and N-glycopeptides. Statistical analysis was performed by WSPP model (Weighted Spectrum, Peptide, and Protein)24. Log2 ratios are expressed as standardized variables, that is, in units of standard deviation (SD) according to their estimated variances (Zq and Zp values for proteins and peptides respectively). Glycoprotein and N-glycopeptide abundance changes between groups were calculated by comparing their Zq and Zp mean values, respectively (C, n =8; HN, n=7). Significant variation was considered if p value <0.05.
keywords: Urine; glycopeptide; chronic kidney disease; early cardiorenal risk;albuminuria

references.referenceLine: Santiago-Hernandez A, Martin-Lorenzo M, Gómez-Serrano M, Lopez JA, Martin-Blazquez A, Vellosillo P, Minguez P, Martinez PJ, Vázquez J, Ruiz-Hurtado G, Barderas MG, Sarafidis P, Segura J, Ruilope LM, Alvarez-Llamas G. The Urinary Glycopeptide Profile Differentiates Early Cardiorenal Risk in Subjects Not Meeting Criteria for Chronic Kidney Disease. Int J Mol Sci. 2024 25(13):7005

Characteristics[Organism]: Hypertensive subjects under chronic renin-angiotensin system (RAS) suppression, with eGFR > 60 mL/min/1.73 m2 and ACR values within the normoalbuminuria range (< 30 mg/g) were recruited from the Hypertension Unit in Hospital 12 de Octubre (Madrid) and classified as the control group (C) if ACR < 10 mg/g or the high-normal group (HN) if ACR = 10–30 mg/g
Characteristics[Age]: Patients between 40 and 75 years were included.
Characteristics[CleavageAgent]: modified trypsin (Sequencing Grade Modified Trypsin; Promega, Maddison, WI, USA)
Characteristics[Label]: 8-plex iTRAQ® reagents (isobaric tags for relative and absolute quantitation, AB Sciex, Nieuwerkerk aan den IJssel, The Netherlands)
Comment[EnrichmentMethod]: wheat germ agglutinin (WGA) lectin (6 µg/µL, L9640, Sigma-Aldrich)
Comment[Separation]: C18 reversed-phase nanocolumn (75 μm ID × 50 cm, 2 μm particle size, Acclaim PepMap RSLC, 100 C18; ThermoFisher Scientific)
Comment[Instrument]: Ultimate 3000 HPLC system (Thermo Scientific, San Jose, CA, USA)
